subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: The login command allows you to authenticate and manage a user account

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: The init command helps you initialize your Appwrite project, functions and collections

subcommands.name: project
subcommands.description: Initialise your Appwrite project

subcommands.name: function
subcommands.description: Initialise your Appwrite cloud function

subcommands.name: collection
subcommands.description: Initialise your Appwrite collections

options.description: Database ID
Flag to initialize all databases

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: The deploy command provides a convenient wrapper for deploying your functions and collections

subcommands.name: function
subcommands.description: Deploy functions in the current directory

options.description: Function ID
Flag to deploy all functions

subcommands.name: collection
subcommands.description: Deploy collections in the current project

options.description: Flag to deploy all functions

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: The logout command allows you to logout of your Appwrite account

subcommands.name: account
subcommands.description: The account command allows you to authenticate and manage a user account

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get currently logged in user data as JSON object

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Use this endpoint to allow a new user to register a new account in your project. After the user registration completes successfully, you can use the [/account/verfication](/docs/client/account#account

options.description: Unique Id. Choose your own unique ID or pass the string "unique()" to auto generate it. Valid chars are a-z, A-Z, 0-9, period, hyphen, and underscore. Can't start with a special char. Max length is 36
User email
User password. Must be at least 8 chars
User name. Max length: 128 chars

subcommands.name: updateEmail
subcommands.description: Update currently logged in user account email address. After changing user address, the user confirmation status will get reset. A new confirmation email is not sent automatically however you can use

options.description: User email
User password. Must be at least 8 chars

subcommands.name: createJWT
subcommands.description: Use this endpoint to create a JSON Web Token. You can use the resulting JWT to authenticate on behalf of the current user when working with the Appwrite server-side API and SDKs. The JWT secret is val

subcommands.name: getLogs
subcommands.description: Get currently logged in user list of latest security activity logs. Each log returns user IP address, location and date and time of log

options.description: Maximum number of logs to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this value to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

subcommands.name: updateName
subcommands.description: Update currently logged in user account name

options.description: User name. Max length: 128 chars

options.description: New user password. Must be at least 8 chars
Current user password. Must be at least 8 chars

subcommands.description: Update the currently logged in user's phone number. After updating the phone number, the phone verification status will be reset. A confirmation SMS is not sent automatically, however you can use the

options.description: Phone number. Format this number with a leading '+' and a country code, e.g., +16175551212
User password. Must be at least 8 chars

subcommands.name: getPrefs
subcommands.description: Get currently logged in user preferences as a key-value object

subcommands.name: updatePrefs
subcommands.description: Update currently logged in user account preferences. The object you pass is stored as is, and replaces any previous value. The maximum allowed prefs size is 64kB and throws error if exceeded

options.description: Prefs key-value JSON object

subcommands.name: createOAuth2Session

options.description: Session ID. Use the string 'current' to delete the current device session

subcommands.description: Block the currently logged in user account. Behind the scene, the user record is not deleted but permanently blocked from any access. To completely delete a user, use the Users API instead

subcommands.name: avatars
subcommands.description: The avatars command aims to help you complete everyday tasks related to your app image, icons, and avatars

subcommands.name: getBrowser
subcommands.description: You can use this endpoint to show different browser icons to your users. The code argument receives the browser code as it appears in your user [GET /account/sessions](/docs/client/account#accountGetS

options.description: Browser Code
Image width. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image height. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image quality. Pass an integer between 0 to 100. Defaults to 100
Output file path

subcommands.description: The credit card endpoint will return you the icon of the credit card provider you need. Use width, height and quality arguments to change the output settings.  When one dimension is specified and the

options.description: Credit Card Code. Possible values: amex, argencard, cabal, censosud, diners, discover, elo, hipercard, jcb, mastercard, naranja, targeta-shopping, union-china-pay, visa, mir, maestro
Image width. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image height. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image quality. Pass an integer between 0 to 100. Defaults to 100
Output file path

subcommands.description: Use this endpoint to fetch the favorite icon (AKA favicon) of any remote website URL

options.description: Website URL which you want to fetch the favicon from
Output file path

options.description: Country Code. ISO Alpha-2 country code format
Image width. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image height. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image quality. Pass an integer between 0 to 100. Defaults to 100
Output file path

Resize preview image width, Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 400

Image width. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Image height. Pass an integer between 0 to 2000. Defaults to 100
Changes text color. By default a random color will be picked and stay will persistent to the given name
Changes background color. By default a random color will be picked and stay will persistent to the given name

subcommands.description: Converts a given plain text to a QR code image. You can use the query parameters to change the size and style of the resulting image

options.description: Plain text to be converted to QR code image
QR code size. Pass an integer between 1 to 1000. Defaults to 400
Margin from edge. Pass an integer between 0 to 10. Defaults to 1

subcommands.name: functions
subcommands.description: The functions command allows you view, create and manage your Cloud Functions

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Get a list of all the project's functions. You can use the query params to filter your results

options.description: Search term to filter your list results. Max length: 256 chars
Maximum number of functions to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this value to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

ID of the function used as the starting point for the query, excluding the function itself. Should be used for efficient pagination when working with large sets of data. [learn more about pagination](
Direction of the cursor, can be either 'before' or 'after'
Order result by ASC or DESC order

subcommands.name: health
subcommands.description: The health command allows you to both validate and monitor your Appwrite server's health

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite HTTP server is up and responsive

subcommands.name: getAntivirus
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite Antivirus server is up and connection is successful

subcommands.name: getCache
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite in-memory cache server is up and connection is successful

subcommands.name: getDB
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite database server is up and connection is successful

subcommands.name: getQueueCertificates
subcommands.description: Get the number of certificates that are waiting to be issued against [Letsencrypt](https://letsencrypt.org/) in the Appwrite internal queue server

subcommands.name: getQueueLogs
subcommands.description: Get the number of logs that are waiting to be processed in the Appwrite internal queue server

subcommands.name: getQueueWebhooks
subcommands.description: Get the number of webhooks that are waiting to be processed in the Appwrite internal queue server

subcommands.name: getStorageLocal
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite local storage device is up and connection is successful

subcommands.name: getTime
subcommands.description: Check the Appwrite server time is synced with Google remote NTP server. We use this technology to smoothly handle leap seconds with no disruptive events. The [Network Time Protocol](https://en.wikiped

subcommands.name: locale
subcommands.description: The locale command allows you to customize your app based on your users' location

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get the current user location based on IP. Returns an object with user country code, country name, continent name, continent code, ip address and suggested currency. You can use the locale header to g

subcommands.name: getContinents
subcommands.description: List of all continents. You can use the locale header to get the data in a supported language

subcommands.name: getCountries
subcommands.description: List of all countries. You can use the locale header to get the data in a supported language

subcommands.name: getCountriesEU
subcommands.description: List of all countries that are currently members of the EU. You can use the locale header to get the data in a supported language

subcommands.name: getCountriesPhones
subcommands.description: List of all countries phone codes. You can use the locale header to get the data in a supported language

subcommands.name: getCurrencies
subcommands.description: List of all currencies, including currency symbol, name, plural, and decimal digits for all major and minor currencies. You can use the locale header to get the data in a supported language

subcommands.name: getLanguages
subcommands.description: List of all languages classified by ISO 639-1 including 2-letter code, name in English, and name in the respective language

subcommands.name: storage
subcommands.description: The storage command allows you to manage your project files

subcommands.name: listBuckets
subcommands.description: Get a list of all the storage buckets. You can use the query params to filter your results

options.description: Search term to filter your list results. Max length: 256 chars
Results limit value. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Results offset. The default value is 0. Use this param to manage pagination
ID of the bucket used as the starting point for the query, excluding the bucket itself. Should be used for efficient pagination when working with large sets of data
Direction of the cursor, can be either 'before' or 'after'
Order result by ASC or DESC order

subcommands.name: createBucket
subcommands.description: Create a new storage bucket

options.description: Unique Id. Choose your own unique ID or pass the string 'unique()' to auto generate it. Valid chars are a-z, A-Z, 0-9, period, hyphen, and underscore. Can't start with a special char. Max length is 36
Bucket name

Permissions type model to use for reading files in this bucket. You can use bucket-level permission set once on the bucket using the 'read' and 'write' params, or you can set file-level permission whe
An array of strings with read permissions. By default no user is granted with any read permissions. [learn more about permissions](/docs/permissions) and get a full list of available permissions
An array of strings with write permissions. By default no user is granted with any write permissions. [learn more about permissions](/docs/permissions) and get a full list of available permissions
Is bucket enabled?

Maximum file size allowed in bytes. Maximum allowed value is 30MB. For self-hosted setups you can change the max limit by changing the '_APP_STORAGE_LIMIT' environment variable. [Learn more about stor
Allowed file extensions. Maximum of 100 extensions are allowed, each 64 characters long
Is encryption enabled? For file size above 20MB encryption is skipped even if it's enabled
Is virus scanning enabled? For file size above 20MB AntiVirus scanning is skipped even if it's enabled

subcommands.name: getBucket
subcommands.description: Get a storage bucket by its unique ID. This endpoint response returns a JSON object with the storage bucket metadata

options.description: Bucket unique ID

subcommands.name: updateBucket
subcommands.description: Update a storage bucket by its unique ID

options.description: Bucket unique ID
Bucket name

Permissions type model to use for reading files in this bucket. You can use bucket-level permission set once on the bucket using the 'read' and 'write' params, or you can set file-level permission whe
An array of strings with read permissions. By default inherits the existing read permissions. [learn more about permissions](/docs/permissions) and get a full list of available permissions
An array of strings with write permissions. By default inherits the existing write permissions. [learn more about permissions](/docs/permissions) and get a full list of available permissions
Is bucket enabled?

Maximum file size allowed in bytes. Maximum allowed value is 30MB. For self hosted version you can change the limit by changing _APP_STORAGE_LIMIT environment variable. [Learn more about storage envir
Allowed file extensions. Maximum of 100 extensions are allowed, each 64 characters long
Is encryption enabled? For file size above 20MB encryption is skipped even if it's enabled
Is virus scanning enabled? For file size above 20MB AntiVirus scanning is skipped even if it's enabled

subcommands.name: deleteBucket
subcommands.description: Delete a storage bucket by its unique ID

options.description: Bucket unique ID

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
Search term to filter your list results. Max length: 256 chars
Maximum number of files to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this param to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

ID of the file used as the starting point for the query, excluding the file itself. Should be used for efficient pagination when working with large sets of data. [learn more about pagination](https://
Direction of the cursor, can be either 'before' or 'after'
Order result by ASC or DESC order

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)

File ID. Choose your own unique ID or pass the string "unique()" to auto generate it. Valid chars are a-z, A-Z, 0-9, period, hyphen, and underscore. Can't start with a special char. Max length is 36 c
Binary file

An array of strings with read permissions. By default only the current user is granted with read permissions. [learn more about permissions](https://appwrite.io/docs/permissions) and get a full list o

An array of strings with write permissions. By default only the current user is granted with write permissions. [learn more about permissions](https://appwrite.io/docs/permissions) and get a full list

subcommands.description: Get a file by its unique ID. This endpoint response returns a JSON object with the file metadata

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File ID

subcommands.description: Update a file by its unique ID. Only users with write permissions have access to update this resource

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File unique ID

An array of strings with read permissions. By default no user is granted with any read permissions. [learn more about permissions](https://appwrite.io/docs/permissions) and get a full list of availabl

An array of strings with write permissions. By default no user is granted with any write permissions. [learn more about permissions](https://appwrite.io/docs/permissions) and get a full list of availa

subcommands.description: Delete a file by its unique ID. Only users with write permissions have access to delete this resource

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File ID

subcommands.name: getFileDownload
subcommands.description: Get a file content by its unique ID. The endpoint response return with a 'Content-Disposition: attachment' header that tells the browser to start downloading the file to user downloads directory

options.description: Storage bucket ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File ID
Output file path

subcommands.name: getFilePreview
subcommands.description: Get a file preview image. Currently, this method supports preview for image files (jpg, png, and gif), other supported formats, like pdf, docs, slides, and spreadsheets, will return the file icon imag

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File ID
Resize preview image width, Pass an integer between 0 to 4000
Resize preview image height, Pass an integer between 0 to 4000
Image crop gravity. Can be one of center,top-left,top,top-right,left,right,bottom-left,bottom,bottom-right
Preview image quality. Pass an integer between 0 to 100. Defaults to 100
Preview image border in pixels. Pass an integer between 0 to 100. Defaults to 0
Preview image border color. Use a valid HEX color, no # is needed for prefix
Preview image border radius in pixels. Pass an integer between 0 to 4000
Preview image opacity. Only works with images having an alpha channel (like png). Pass a number between 0 to 1
Preview image rotation in degrees. Pass an integer between -360 and 360
Preview image background color. Only works with transparent images (png). Use a valid HEX color, no # is needed for prefix
Output format type (jpeg, jpg, png, gif and webp)
Output file path

subcommands.name: getFileView
subcommands.description: Get a file content by its unique ID. This endpoint is similar to the download method but returns with no  'Content-Disposition: attachment' header

options.description: Storage bucket unique ID. You can create a new storage bucket using the Storage service [server integration](/docs/server/storage#createBucket)
File ID
Output file path
Date range
Bucket ID
Date range

subcommands.name: teams
subcommands.description: The teams command allows you to group users of your project and to enable them to share read and write access to your project resources

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description:
Get a list of all the teams in which the current user is a member. You can use the parameters to filter your results.
 
 In admin mode, this endpoint returns a list of all the teams in the current pro

options.description: Search term to filter your list results. Max length: 256 chars
Maximum number of teams to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this param to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

ID of the team used as the starting point for the query, excluding the team itself. Should be used for efficient pagination when working with large sets of data. [learn more about pagination](https://
Direction of the cursor, can be either 'before' or 'after'
Order result by ASC or DESC order

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new team. The user who creates the team will automatically be assigned as the owner of the team. Only the users with the owner role can invite new members, add new owners and delete or update

options.description: Team ID. Choose your own unique ID or pass the string "unique()" to auto generate it. Valid chars are a-z, A-Z, 0-9, period, hyphen, and underscore. Can't start with a special char. Max length is 36 c
Team name. Max length: 128 chars

Array of strings. Use this param to set the roles in the team for the user who created it. The default role is **owner**. A role can be any string. Learn more about [roles and permissions](/docs/permi

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get a team by its ID. All team members have read access for this resource

options.description: Team ID

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update a team using its ID. Only members with the owner role can update the team

options.description: Team ID
New team name. Max length: 128 chars

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a team using its ID. Only team members with the owner role can delete the team

options.description: Team ID

subcommands.name: listLogs
subcommands.description: Get the team activity logs list by its unique ID

options.description: Team ID
Maximum number of logs to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this value to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

subcommands.name: getMemberships
subcommands.description: Use this endpoint to list a team's members using the team's ID. All team members have read access to this endpoint

options.description: Team ID
Search term to filter your list results. Max length: 256 chars
Maximum number of memberships to return in response. By default will return maximum 25 results. Maximum of 100 results allowed per request
Offset value. The default value is 0. Use this value to manage pagination. [learn more about pagination](https://appwrite.io/docs/pagination)

ID of the membership used as the starting point for the query, excluding the membership itself. Should be used for efficient pagination when working with large sets of data. [learn more about paginati
Direction of the cursor, can be either 'before' or 'after'
Order result by ASC or DESC order

Email of the new team member

Array of strings. Use this param to set the user roles in the team. A role can be any string. Learn more about [roles and permissions](/docs/permissions). Maximum of 100 roles are allowed, each 32 cha

URL to redirect the user back to your app from the invitation email.  Only URLs from hostnames in your project platform list are allowed. This requirement helps to prevent an [open redirect](https://c
Name of the new team member. Max length: 128 chars

subcommands.description: Get a team member by the membership unique id. All team members have read access for this resource
